scope: Farm environment kit sub-palettes only (forest, wood_leather, metal, arcane). Character sub-palettes such as skin_hair and ui_neutral are locked elsewhere when their own production art lands and are intentionally out of scope here.

wood_leather.role: dirt path, fences, gates, signposts, crates, logs

arcane.role: pond water (cool blues) and magical accents (violets, cyan glow)

pinnedAccents._source: Locked by src/presentation/WildbloomDiscoveryController.ts WILDBLOOM_SPOTS; these are authoritative gameplay values and must not drift.

notes: Specification only; no art is included.

This is the concrete hex lock the visual contract Section 4 requires before farm production art is committed, so independently generated assets (e.g. via an external pixel-art generator) cannot silently drift on what 'forest' or 'arcane' mean. It is versioned (v1): revising a color is an explicit, reviewable palette change, not silent drift.

Ramps are ordered dark-to-light; the artist/generator applies the contract's upper-left key light so highlights land upper-left and shadows fall down-right. Do not bake a scene-level warm/gold ambient tint into source art (contract Section 14 applies that once, at the scene level).
The soft ground shadow under dynamic actors/props (contract Section 8a) is engine-drawn, not a palette color; it is intentionally not listed here.
